title: AI Builders Digest
subtitle: Bilingual edition · 双语对照版

intro.text: 今天的主线是 Microsoft Build 之后,大家突然在讲同一件事:harness。Satya 在 No Priors 把这层意思说得很直白——真正的护城河不是哪个模型,而是你怎么把模型、数据、工具、eval 装进一个 harness 里,让自己能在 frontier 上持续 hill climb。Steipete 在 Build 上演讲的题目就叫[build the thing that builds the thing],Cat Wu 同步发了 Anthropic 数据团队用 Claude 自动化 95% 业务分析的实战贴,Anthropic 自己也放出了[How we contain Claude]工程长文——三件事从不同方向都在说同一件事:agent 已经从 demo 阶段过渡到生产阶段,harness 才是值班的人。

archive.title: 底座上桌日报
archive.desc: Build 之后业界在讲同一件事:harness、eval、context 才是新护城河,模型本身反倒变成可替换件。

footerNote: Source: Follow Builders curated daily digest. Rebuilt from the 2026-05-29 source feeds.

sections.title: Harness Becomes the Moat / Harness 成为新护城河
sections.desc: Build 之后,三家独立说同一件事:模型层会变成可替换件,harness + eval + context 才是真正决定谁能上 frontier 的那层。

rewrite: Steipete's Build talk is titled Build the thing that builds the thing — and the video is now public. The framing matters: he is not pitching OpenClaw as a product, he is pitching it as a meta-tool that other builders will use to build their own products.

The talk lands the same week Anthropic ships the [How we contain Claude] engineering post and Satya tells No Priors that the third act of Microsoft is the harness company. Three independent sources, one thesis: the value capture moves up the stack, away from raw tokens.
236 likes / 35 RTs for a Build talk recording is the kind of engagement reserved for founders who are naming a new category in real time.
original: Here's the video of my talk at MS Build: Build the thing that builds the thing.

rewrite: Steipete 在 Build 上的演讲视频放出来了,题目就叫[build the thing that builds the thing]——造那个能造东西的东西。他不是在卖 OpenClaw 这个产品,他是在卖一种方法:让别的 builder 用 OpenClaw 去造他们自己的产品。
这场演讲跟 Anthropic 同周放出的[How we contain Claude]工程长文、Satya 在 No Priors 上说[微软第三幕是 harness 公司]形成三角呼应——三家不同来源指向同一个判断:价值捕获正在向 stack 上面那一层迁移,远离最底层的 token。
236 赞、35 转推——这种量级的 Build 演讲录播数据,通常是有人在现场命名了一个新类目。
original: Steipete 在 MS Build 上讲:Build the thing that builds the thing。造那个造东西的东西。

rewrite: Cat Wu just shipped the Anthropic data team's playbook: 95% of business analytics queries are now automated through Claude. The post is the public version of the harness discipline Anthropic has been building internally — evals, ablations, online validation.

The interesting detail: this is not a single agent, it is an eval-gated pipeline where a generated SQL query has to clear a confidence bar before it ships. The harness decides what the model is allowed to do, not the other way around.
280 likes for a long-form engineering writeup is high signal — most of the builder community is still chasing prompts, while the production teams have already moved on to harness design.
original: Excited to share how Anthropic's data team has automated 95% of business analytics queries with Claude. Blog post covers how we approach evals, ablations, and online validation!

rewrite: Cat Wu 放出 Anthropic 数据团队的实战贴:95% 的业务分析查询现在由 Claude 自动化。文章本身就是内部 harness 纪律的公开版——evals、ablation、在线验证,全都有。
关键细节:这并不是一个单 agent,而是一条带 eval 门槛的流水线——生成的 SQL 必须先过一道置信度闸口才能上生产。Harness 决定模型能做什么,不是反过来。
280 赞对一篇工程长文来说是非常高的信号——大多数 builder 还在拼 prompt,生产团队早就转到 harness 设计了。
original: Anthropic 数据团队 95% 业务分析查询由 Claude 自动化,核心是 evals / ablations / online validation 的 harness 设计。

rewrite: Anthropic engineering just published the longest, most honest post we have seen from them on agent security: How we contain Claude across products. The throughline: blast radius is the new risk metric, and containment is the discipline, not supervision.

The most quotable line: a year ago they would have rejected the access levels Claude now routinely holds. The shift is not philosophical — it is empirical. Users approve ~93% of permission prompts, which means human-in-the-loop supervision is structurally weaker than it looks.

Read this with Steipete's Build talk and Satya's harness thesis. All three are saying the same thing from different angles: the agent is already in production, the question is no longer if, it is how you cap the damage when it goes wrong.
original: Twelve months ago, we'd have rejected out of hand the idea of granting Claude access sufficient to take down an internal Anthropic service. Today that level of access is routine, and Anthropic developers are more productive for it.

rewrite: Anthropic 工程团队放出了迄今最长、最坦诚的一篇 agent 安全长文:How we contain Claude across products。主线一句话:风险指标换成了 blast radius,纪律换成了 containment,不是 supervision。
最值得引用的一句:一年前他们会直接拒绝给 Claude 那种能干掉自家内部服务的权限,如今这种权限已是常态。这个转变不是哲学层面的,是数据驱动的——用户对权限弹窗的通过率是 93%,人审循环的结构性弱点被量化出来了。
跟 Steipete 的 Build 演讲、Satya 的 harness 论放在一起读,三件事是同一个判断的三个面:agent 已经在生产里了,问题不再是上不上,是你怎么在它出错时把伤害圈起来。
original: 一年前 Anthropic 会拒绝给 Claude 那么大的内部权限,如今这种权限已是常态,开发者反而更高效。风险结构变了,blast radius 成为新指标。

sections.title: The Full-Stack Generalist Era / 全栈通用型人才登场
sections.desc: Satya 的 podcast、Cursor 的招聘、YC 投资人的招聘——三股力量同步说:generalist 的杠杆在 agent 时代被放到了最大。

rewrite: Ryo Lu is hiring design engineers at Cursor. The job description reads like a LinkedIn full-stack builder ad: taste, systems thinking, deep care for fast polished experiences — and explicitly, people excited to build the tools that help designers, engineers, and agents ship quality code.

The interesting last clause: agents are now in the same sentence as designers and engineers. Cursor is not just hiring for the human + AI handoff, they are hiring for the agent-tooling product surface itself.
1012 likes for a single job posting — this is the most-upvoted [we are hiring] tweet of the week, and the role is a near-textbook match for Satya's generalist thesis.
original: Cursor is hiring design engineers. looking for people with taste, systems thinking, and deep care for fast, polished experiences – especially folks excited to build the tools that help designers, engineers, and agents ship quality code. know someone? tag them or DM me.

rewrite: Ryo Lu 在帮 Cursor 招 design engineer。JD 几乎是 LinkedIn full-stack builder 范本的复刻:品味、系统思维、对快速精致体验的执念——并且明确说要招愿意造[帮设计师、工程师、agent 一起出货]的工具的人。
最有信息量的是最后那句:agent 已经和设计师、工程师并列。Cursor 招的不只是人机协作的衔接岗,他们是在招 agent 工具链本身的产品面。
1012 赞,本周点赞最高的招聘帖——而这个岗位几乎是 Satya 通用型人才论的标准答卷。
original: Cursor 招 design engineer,核心是[造让设计师、工程师、agent 一起出货的工具],agent 已经跟人并列。

rewrite: Aditya Agarwal (ex-Facebook eng, ex-Dropbox CTO, now South Park Commons GP) is hiring — but the names are worth more than the headline. Harshit Madan and Rohan Choudhary joining the team.

The unstated point: even at the GP / fund-of-funds layer, the partners are signaling that their next hires are operators, not analysts. The generalist operator is back, this time with an AI deployment budget.

Low engagement (20 likes) but a quiet signal: when ex-CTO VCs start hiring, the venture model is shifting to operator-led builds, which is the same generalist thesis Satya was making on the enterprise side.
original: Very excited to welcome Harshit Madan and Rohan Choudhary to the team!

rewrite: Aditya Agarwal(Facebook 早期工程、Dropbox 前 CTO、South Park Commons GP)在招人——真正有信息量的是名字:Harshit Madan 和 Rohan Choudhary 入伙。
潜台词是:哪怕在 GP / 基金层,合伙人下一波招的是 operator,不是 analyst。通用型 operator 重新吃香,这次还带 AI 部署预算。
20 赞的数字很低,但信号很静:前 CTO 转 VC 开始招 operator,意味着投资模型正在向 operator-led build 倾斜,跟 Satya 在企业端说的通用型人才论是同一回事。
original: 前 CTO 出身的 VC 招 operator 入伙,投资模型正在向 operator-led build 倾斜。

rewrite: Garry Tan's most-shared tweet of the week is a short, almost throwaway line: San Francisco is healing. We don't fall for this type of ridiculous gaslighting anymore. 2354 likes, 98 RTs.

Read in the full-stack builder context: the implicit claim is that the city that incubates the agent build wave is back as the place to be. YC has been quietly rotating its portfolio toward agentic infrastructure — this tweet is the cultural marker that the broader SF ecosystem has accepted the rotation.
It is not an AI tweet, it is a Builder-attitude tweet. The generalist thesis needs a place; SF is reaffirming it is that place.
original: San Francisco is healing. We don't fall for this type of ridiculous gaslighting anymore.

rewrite: Garry Tan 本周被转最多的一条推很短,几乎是随口一句:San Francisco is healing. We don't fall for this type of ridiculous gaslighting anymore.2354 赞,98 转推。
放在全栈 Builder 的语境里读:潜台词是,孵化这一波 agent build 的城市重新成了 place to be。YC 一直在悄悄把 portfolio 向 agentic infra 倾斜,这条推是更广的 SF 生态接受这个转向的文化标记。
这不是一条 AI 推,是 Builder 心态推。通用型人才论需要一座城市,SF 在重新声明它就是那座。
original: Garry Tan:旧金山在自愈,agent build 一波的城市重新成为 place to be。

sections.title: Codex Stability Reset & the Long Game / Codex 复位与长线
sections.desc: Thibault 主动认错三次事故、给所有付费 plan 复位 usage limit;Peter Yang 在另一边狂夸 Codex 编辑表格的体验。Codex 在快速长大。

rewrite: Thibault Sottiaux (head of Codex) just posted a public mea culpa: three small incidents in 24 hours affected Codex reliability, three too many. He reset usage limits across all paid plans.

The interesting framing is [vectors all pointing in the same direction] in his other tweet of the day — incidents are not random, they are a sign that every part of the stack is being pushed at once. The reset is the right product call, the apology is the right community call.
8735 likes on the reliability post — readers reward founders who name their own failures, especially right before a major Microsoft Build cycle.
original: Hi. Over the last 24 hours we had three separate small incidents that affected Codex reliability. Those are three too many and we are taking active steps for them to not reproduce. I have reset usage limits for Codex across all paid plans. May the tokens flow again.

rewrite: Thibault Sottiaux(Codex 负责人)公开发了一条认错推:过去 24 小时出了三起影响 Codex 可靠性的事故,三次都太多。给所有付费 plan 复了 usage limit。
同一天的另一条推里他说[lots of little vectors all pointing in the same direction]——事故不是随机的,是 stack 上每个点都在被一起推。复 limit 是正确的产品动作,公开认错是正确的社区动作。
8735 赞给一条可靠性贴——读者在奖励[自己点自己名]的创始人,尤其是在 Microsoft Build 周期前夜。
original: Thibault 主动认 24 小时内 3 次 Codex 事故,所有付费 plan 复位 usage limit。May the tokens flow again。

rewrite: Peter Yang on Codex editing spreadsheets: it is absolutely killing it this afternoon. The framing matters — he is using Codex for end-user productivity (spreadsheet editing) not coding, which echoes the OpenAI report Zara surfaced last week about knowledge workers overtaking developers on Codex adoption.
The second tweet is the quieter but more important one: he wants to make Codex the default tab when he opens ChatGPT. The default-tab ask is the leading indicator of agent becoming OS-level surface.

Read together with Thibault's reliability post: the product is good enough that the user wants it pinned to the launch surface, and the team is now scrambling to make sure the reliability matches the demand.
original: Codex is absolutely killing it this afternoon for editing spreadsheets for me.

rewrite: Peter Yang 评 Codex:今天下午用它改表格,绝对在杀。重要的是用法——他拿 Codex 干的是终端用户的事(改表格),不是写代码,跟 Zara 上周拎出来的[知识工作者在 Codex 上反超开发者]那条 OpenAI 报告对得上。
他当天另一条推更有信息量:他想把 Codex 设成打开 ChatGPT 时的默认 tab。默认 tab 这个请求,是 agent 变成 OS 级表面的领先指标。
跟 Thibault 的可靠性推对读:产品已经好到用户想把它钉在启动面,团队现在要拼的是让可靠性追上这个需求。
original: Peter Yang:Codex 今天下午改表格杀疯了,另一条推想把它设成 ChatGPT 默认 tab。

rewrite: Second Thibault tweet of the day, the upbeat counterpart to the reliability apology: lots of little vectors at OpenAI all pointing in the same direction, excited to see them add up over the coming weeks.

Read this against the Microsoft Build context: Codex is on the verge of getting a major surface lift, and Thibault is preparing the audience for it. The two tweets together — sorry for the bumps, here is the runway — is the most disciplined comms pattern a product lead can run during a launch cycle.
760 likes for a 'stay tuned' tweet is unusually high engagement; the audience is clearly expecting something concrete soon.
original: Lots of little vectors at OpenAI all pointing in the same direction. Excited to see it all add up and come together over the coming weeks.

rewrite: Thibault 当天第二条推,跟可靠性认错那条是阴阳面:OpenAI 内部很多小 vector 都指向同一方向,期待未来几周一起加总。
放在 Microsoft Build 的语境里读:Codex 即将有一次大的 surface 提升,Thibault 在给观众做预告。这两条推一起看——为颠簸道歉、这是 runway——是一个产品负责人在发布周期里能跑的最有纪律的 comms 节奏。
760 赞给一条 stay-tuned,是非常高的预期值,观众显然在等一个具体的事。
original: Thibault:OpenAI 内部小 vector 都同向,期待未来几周加总——给 Build 前后的 Codex 升级做预告。

sections.title: OpenClaw's Real-World Climb / OpenClaw 实战爬升
sections.desc: OpenClaw 一周 npm 下载量冲到历史新高、线上活动候补 1300 人、Anthropic 官号亲自转了一个 Clawd 实体机器人——生态从社区变成产品。

rewrite: Steipete's biggest tweet of the week: OpenClaw had more npm downloads this week than ever, and the combined number across Docker, GitHub, internal deployments, and forks puts the real figure at 10-20 million downloads per week.

This is the kind of distribution number that makes a developer tool stop being a hobby and start being infrastructure. OpenClaw is no longer a cool project — it is a load-bearing layer for a meaningful slice of the agent build community.
602 likes / 29 RTs — the engagement matches the size of the claim. The 10-20M/week figure is the headline every agent infrastructure company benchmarks against now.
original: We never had more npm downloads than this week on @openclaw - comined with Docker, GitHub, company-internal deployments and the numerous forks, real number is more in the 10-20 million downloads/week.

rewrite: Steipete 本周最大的一条推:OpenClaw 一周 npm 下载量历史新高,加上 Docker、GitHub、企业内部部署和大量 fork,真实数字在 1000-2000 万次/周。
这个量级的分发数据,意味着一个开发者工具从爱好项目正式变成基础设施。OpenClaw 已经不是[酷项目],它已经是有意义比例的 agent build 社区在用的承重层。
602 赞、29 转推——互动量跟声明的体量匹配。1000-2000 万次/周,成了现在所有 agent infra 公司对标的基准线。
original: Steipete:OpenClaw 一周 npm 下载量历史新高,合计 Docker/GitHub/企业部署/fork 真实数字 1000-2000 万次/周。

rewrite: Second Steipete: 1300+ people on the waitlist for today's OpenClaw event, livestreamed on Twitch and Discord for everyone else. The capacity is the story — a developer-tool community event is filling a meetup-sized room and an async-fallback audience at once.

This is a brand maturing in real time. The audience is no longer [Steipete's followers], it is [the OpenClaw community] — and that is the moment any tool crosses from personal project to category leader.
206 likes / 16 RTs — quiet, but the 1300 number is the load-bearing fact.
original: We have over 1300 people on the waitlist for today's OpenClaw event - will be livestreamed on Twitch and Discord tho!

rewrite: Steipete 第二条:今天 OpenClaw 活动候补名单 1300+ 人,其他人在 Twitch 和 Discord 看直播。容量本身就是新闻——一个开发者工具的社区活动,候补规模已经到了线下 meetup 的体量,加上异步兜底观众一起算,这是一个正在长大的场。
这是一个品牌实时成熟的标志:受众不再是[Steipete 的关注者],是[OpenClaw 社区]——这是任何工具从个人项目跨到品类 leader 的那一刻。
206 赞、16 转推——数字很静,但 1300 这个候补量才是承重事实。
original: OpenClaw 活动候补 1300+ 人,Twitch+Discord 直播兜底——社区已经独立于创始人存在。

rewrite: Anthropic's official Claude account just amplified a maker project: Ben built a physical Clawd (the lobster mascot) that jumps up and down on a solenoid when Claude Code needs user input. The body is machined aluminum; an Arduino reads intercepted keyboard signals; a Raspberry Pi Zero handles the brain.

The signal is not the build (it is a charming tinker toy) — the signal is Anthropic amplifying it. The Claude team is publicly celebrating the [agent as physical presence] aesthetic, which is a soft product direction signal for what comes after the chat box.
112 likes for the build post and 95 for the video — small numbers, but Anthropic's amplification is the real distribution event.
original: Ben kept getting distracted during long Claude Code sessions, so he built a miniature Clawd to get his attention. When Claude Code needs input, a solenoid inside the aluminum body pushes it upward. In other words, it jumps up and down.

rewrite: Anthropic 官号 Claude 亲自转了一个 maker 项目:Ben 做了一个实体的 Clawd(龙虾吉祥物)——Claude Code 需要用户输入时,壳里的 solenoid 会把它顶起来,等于[跳起来叫你回来]。壳体是机加工铝,Arduino 拦截键盘信号,Raspberry Pi Zero 负责大脑。
信号点不在作品本身(它是个可爱的玩具),在 Anthropic 亲自转它。Claude 团队在公开嘉许[agent 作为物理存在]的美学,这是对[chat 框之后是什么]的一个软性产品方向信号。
112 赞 + 95 赞——数字不大,但 Anthropic 的官号放大才是真正的分发事件。
original: Anthropic 官号亲自转 Clawd 实体机器人:agent 已经从屏幕里走出来变成物理在场。

sections.title: The New Mobile Agent Surface / 新移动端面
sections.desc: Google Labs 推出 Dreambeans——一个[希望型滚动,不是末日型滚动]的移动端;Nikunj 在另一边琢磨 Fitbit Air。移动端在 agent 时代重新被想起。

rewrite: Google Labs launched a new experimental mobile app: Dreambeans. The pitch is Personal Intelligence — a daily collection of personalized stories that surface things you might otherwise miss, surfacing what is relevant to you and letting you dive deeper into topics that matter.

The framing is the opposite of doom-scrolling: hope-scrolling. The hallway pitch that got the team the green light. This is Google doing what Google does best — taking the infinite scroll pattern and re-stapling it to a positive-sum purpose, now with an agent pulling the content from your other Google apps.
1057 likes / 97 RTs — Google Labs rarely ships consumer product surprises anymore; the engagement is the surprise.
original: Dreambeans is a new, experimental mobile app that uses Personal Intelligence to connect to your Google apps. Every day, it delivers collections of personalized stories, surfacing things you might otherwise miss, alongside topics that are relevant to you, to help you dive deeper into topics.

rewrite: Google Labs 推出实验性新移动应用:Dreambeans。卖点是 Personal Intelligence——每天给你一组个性化故事,把你可能错过的内容捞出来,同时给相关主题一个往下挖的入口。
框架是 doom-scrolling 的反义:hope-scrolling。希望型滚动。这是 Google 最擅长的事——把无限滚动的范式重新钉到正和博弈的目的上,这次由一个 agent 从你其他 Google app 里拉内容。
1057 赞、97 转推——Google Labs 很少再出消费侧产品惊喜,这次互动量本身就是惊喜。
original: Google Labs 推 Dreambeans:Personal Intelligence + 希望型滚动,从你其他 Google app 里捞内容做每日个性化故事。

rewrite: Josh Woodward's backstory tweet is the longer read: a small Google Labs team pitched [Hope scrolling, not doom scrolling] in a hallway and got the [go for it] answer. Today that little experiment is rolling out as Dreambeans.

501 likes / 49 RTs — by far the highest-engagement tweet in the Google Labs thread. The reason is the framing: the entire post is a defense of small internal teams shipping opinionated consumer products inside a giant company.

The agent-era signal: the labs model is back. Bigcos are re-allowing small teams to ship opinionated surfaces because the cost of building a new mobile experience is now low enough that the experiment pays for itself.
original: A short backstory on this one: A small Google Labs team had an idea to make an app designed to connect you with what matters, without the endless scroll. "Hope scrolling, not doom scrolling" was the hallway pitch. "Go for it." And today, that little experiment is rolling out. Meet Dreambeans.

rewrite: Josh Woodward 的那条 backstory 推是更长的版本:一个小 Google Labs 团队在走廊里 pitch 了[希望型滚动,不是末日型滚动],得到一句[go for it]。今天这个小实验作为 Dreambeans 正式发布。
501 赞、49 转推——是整个 Google Labs 帖里互动最高的一条。原因在框架:全文是一次为[大公司里小团队做有立场的产品]的辩护。
Agent 时代的信号:labs 模式回归。大公司重新允许小团队做有立场的 surface,因为造一个新移动体验的成本已经低到实验本身能自己回本。
original: Josh Woodward 公开 Dreambeans 的内部故事:[Hope scrolling, not doom scrolling] 走廊 pitch,得到 go for it——大公司里小团队做有立场产品的胜利。

rewrite: Nikunj Kothari (FPV Ventures, ex-Meter early hire) on the Fitbit Air: [Professional at the top, monitoring at the bottom]. The hardware wedge he wants: Google Health API on the watch, write capabilities into Apple Health, and a nice band.

The interesting detail is the API request buried in the product take — the consumer agent story will not get built on the back of one vendor's stack. It needs the same cross-platform handoff Cat Wu is doing for analytics.
40 likes is a soft number, but Nikunj is a real seed/A investor; his [what I would buy] tweet is a public market-making signal for the cross-platform personal-health-agent stack.
original: Getting on this Fitbit Air hype train. Professional at the top, monitoring at the bottom. Now just need Google Health API, write capabilities to Apple Health and a nice band and we're all set!

rewrite: Nikunj Kothari(FPV Ventures,前 Meter 早期)聊 Fitbit Air:[上面专业感,下面监测感]。他想看到的硬件楔子是:Google Health API 写到表里、写权限到 Apple Health、再加一条好看的表带。
藏在产品点评里的是 API 请求——消费侧 agent 故事不会只靠单一供应商 stack 撑起来,需要 Cat Wu 那种跨平台 handoff 模式。
40 赞是个软数字,但 Nikunj 是真 seed/A 投资人,他这条[我会买什么]推,本质是对[跨平台个人健康 agent stack]的一次公开做市。
original: Nikunj:Fitbit Air 上面专业下面监测,但需要 Google Health API + Apple Health 写权限 + 跨平台 handoff。

sections.title: Cost Story & Frontend Generation / 成本与前端生成
sections.desc: Levie 重新算 AI 时代的软件开支:Rauch 用 V0 把 Snowflake 数据直接渲染成前端——成本结构在被改写,工具分层在被重新画。

rewrite: Aaron Levie's most-quoted tweet of the week: even with employer caps, AI token spend dramatically exceeds any other historical software spend. Companies used to pay $10-50 per employee per month for software; now they pay hundreds or thousands on tokens to augment productivity.

The cost-per-employee number is the new CFO question. The historical SaaS budget framework (per-seat, capped, predictable) is structurally wrong for an agent-augmented workforce. Procurement teams will spend the next 18 months rewriting the budget model.

219 likes / 21 RTs — Levie is now the de facto public commentator on the enterprise AI budget reframe. The number — hundreds to thousands of dollars per employee per month on tokens — is the new baseline.
original: Even with employer caps, the spend on AI tokens dramatically exceeds any other historical spend on software. Typically, companies maybe would spend on the order of $10-50 for a software license per month per employee, but now will pay hundreds or thousands on tokens to augment their productivity.

rewrite: Aaron Levie 本周被引最多的一条:就算加了雇主上限,AI token 支出也大幅超过历史上任何软件支出。以前公司每月给每个员工付 10-50 美元软件许可,现在给 token 付几百到几千美元去放大产能。
人/单月成本数字是 CFO 的新问题。历史 SaaS 预算框架(按席位、封顶、可预测)在结构上就不适合 agent 增强型 workforce。采购团队接下来 18 个月要重写预算模型。
219 赞、21 转推——Levie 已经成为企业 AI 预算再框架的公开评论员。每个员工每月几百到几千美元 token,是新基准线。
original: Levie:即使有雇主上限,AI token 支出也远超历史软件支出——人/月从 10-50 美元 SaaS 变成几百到几千美元 token。

rewrite: Second Levie tweet, the labor-market rebuttal: the jobs data continues to suggest the opposite of what a lot of people thought would happen. Engineering, as the prime example of the AI-impacted area, now has far more software projects than ever before because of AI.

The argument is the productivity-J-curve applied to engineering: AI compresses the marginal cost of a project, so companies start more projects, and the engineer headcount ceiling moves up rather than down. Same logic Box's customers are reporting in content workflows.
358 likes / 49 RTs — the tweet of the week that most product builders are screenshotting and forwarding to their worried engineering managers.
original: The jobs data coming out continues to suggest the opposite of what a lot of people had thought would happen. Just take engineering, as the prime example of the area with greatest AI impact (and perceived risk). Most companies now have far more software projects than ever before because of AI, and the headcount ceiling moves up, not down.

rewrite: Levie 第二条,就业数据反驳:就业数据继续跟很多人以为的相反。拿工程岗举例——AI 影响最大、感知风险最高的领域——公司现在因为 AI 启动了比以往更多的软件项目。
论点是把生产力 J 曲线套到工程上:AI 压低了一个项目的边际成本,公司就会启动更多项目,工程师人头天花板往上走,不是往下。Box 的客户在内容工作流里报的是同一个故事。
358 赞、49 转推——本周被 builder 截图转发给焦虑工程经理最多的一条。
original: Levie:就业数据反向——AI 让工程岗启动的项目比以往更多,人头天花板往上不是往下。

rewrite: Guillermo Rauch: generating frontends on top of your business data is one of the killer apps of coding AI. Vercel already ran on Snowflake; with V0 and Next.js, they are now getting 1000x the value out of the same data layer.

The 1000x claim is the headline — it is a Vercel-self-reported number, but the directional point is correct: a structured semantic layer + an LLM in the middle + a frontend generator on top turns a static dashboard into an on-demand surface.

268 likes / 11 RTs — Vercel is publicly claiming the [data-to-frontend] agent pipeline. The implication for SaaS is the same one Satya made on the podcast: dashboards are getting unbundled and rebundled by agents.
original: Generating frontends on top of your business data is one of the killer apps of coding AI. The genie is out of the bottle. Never going back to clunky and rigid dashboards. Vercel already ran on Snowflake. But with V0 and Next.js, we're now getting 1000x the value.

rewrite: Guillermo Rauch:在业务数据之上生成前端,是 coding AI 的一类 killer app。Vercel 早就跑在 Snowflake 上,有了 V0 和 Next.js,他们从同一层数据里拿到的价值涨了 1000 倍。
1000 倍这个数字是头条——它是 Vercel 自报数,但方向是对的:结构化语义层 + 中间一个 LLM + 上面前端生成器,把静态 dashboard 变成按需 surface。
268 赞、11 转推——Vercel 公开认领了[数据到前端]的 agent 流水线。对 SaaS 的含义跟 Satya 在 podcast 里说的是同一件:dashboard 正在被 agent 拆开再重新打包。
original: Rauch:V0 + Next.js 让 Vercel 在同一层 Snowflake 数据上拿到 1000 倍价值——dashboard 正在被 agent 拆开重打。

sections.title: Podcast: Satya Nadella on Full-Stack Builders / 播客:Satya 谈全栈 Builder
sections.desc: No Priors 这一期请到了 Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella:从 Azure 网络运维怎么用 agent 化、到 MAI 模型的 lineage,再到[第三幕是 harness 公司],他给企业 AI 时代画了一整张图。

rewrite: Satya Nadella on No Priors, the headline frame: the third act of Microsoft is the harness company. The thesis is that the value capture moves from the model to the harness — the integration of model + data + tools + private evals that lets a company hill-climb on frontier intelligence.

He is unusually candid about underestimating deployment complexity. The crude line — [intelligence is log of compute, kind of works] — is true for benchmarks; the real eval is what users can do in the real world, and that is much harder than the benchmarks suggested.

The best business-model moment: pricing is moving from per-user to per-usage because per-user was always an artifact of buyer wanting budget certainty, not a reflection of how the value is actually produced.
original: Our job is not to do Azure networking. Our job is to build the agentic system that does Azure networking.
I think the third act of Microsoft historically, as an operating systems company and then become a cloud company, maybe, like, the third act is that you're a harness or eval's company.
per user pricing is really an artifact of someone creating a budget needing certainty.

rewrite: Satya Nadella 上 No Priors,主线一句话:微软的第三幕是 harness 公司。论点是价值捕获从模型层搬到 harness——把模型、数据、工具、私域 evals 装到一起,让一家公司能在 frontier intelligence 上持续 hill climb。
他罕见地承认低估了部署的复杂度。粗一点的说法是[intelligence is log of compute,差不多对]——这句话对 benchmark 成立;真评估是用户在真实世界里能做出什么,这比 benchmark 暗示的难得多。
商业模式那一段最好:定价正在从 per-user 走向 per-usage,因为 per-user 一直是买家想要预算确定性的人造产物,不是真实价值产生方式的反映。
original: Satya:我们的活不是跑 Azure 网络,是建那个跑 Azure 网络的 agentic 系统。
微软的第三幕是 harness / evals 公司。
Per-user 定价是预算确定性需求的人造产物,不是价值本身的形状。
